name: Automated late-payment reminder email
description: Invoice is 7 days past due. System sends a polite reminder on the contractor's behalf. Reminder cadence is bounded (no spam) and the contractor can override or pause it.

persona.name: Mike Park
persona.context: Forgot about an invoice — life happens.

preconditions: Reminder cadence enabled in Diego's settings: 7d, 14d, 30d

steps.action: At day 7, generates and queues a reminder email
steps.expect: Email is friendly, not aggressive; from Diego's name; reuses the original public invoice link
Subject e.g. 'Quick reminder — invoice for fence repair'; body opens with 'Hi Mike, just a friendly nudge…'
Future reminders for this invoice are cancelled

steps.action: (Diego) opens /invoices and pauses reminders for a different overdue invoice
steps.expect: Toggle on the invoice; backend marks reminders disabled; no further emails sent for that invoice

emails.subject: Quick reminder — invoice for fence repair
emails.expect: Tone is human; no bold red text; signed off in Diego's voice

evaluationCriteria.check: Email is visually identical to the original invoice email — familiar, not 'collections department'
Reminder feels like a person nudging, not a debt collector
Reminders respect the contractor's per-account cadence config; suppressed when invoice is paid or cancelled
All key info (amount, due date, link) present in plain text body for non-HTML email clients
